reviews.5267354:
Before he scored movies, Randy Newman was writing unusual, sardonic songs.

More often than not, his songs from this period are an indictment of the ugliness lurking below the surface (and sometimes in plain sight) in American society.

Standout track: Sail Away
reviews.15277229:
10cc have often struck me as being a kind of analogous British alternative universe version of Steely Dan. Absolute studio weapons, songwriting mostly driven by a pair of musicians, with their songwriting and lyrics driven just as much by the ideas of art and cinema as anything else. 

Like Steely Dan, they seemed to stretch the limits of what could fit into the bucket of 'popular rock' - prog-like time signature shifts, jazzy minor chord progression, deeply layered vocal harmonies, sardonic lyrics. 

I can't go past the album closer, the 11:34 opus Feel The Benefit - (Pts. 1, 2 & 3). "Feel the benefit" is stretched from a typical British idiom to pondering about how the artists of the world justify their existence, to dreams of universal harmony. Incredibly lush orchestration, amazing solos, a mid-song Latin break - what more could you want?

Standout track: Feel The Benefit - (Pts. 1, 2 & 3)
reviews.31793180:
I've been a fan of Photay since I heard his self-titled EP, released on Astro Nautico in 2014. He's continued to develop his experimental electronic sound, with its incorporation of field recordings, synthesisers and live instrumentation. All of his compositions are interesting, organic, original and deeply human.

Standout track: Thermal Loop
